text.title: adobe-firefly-integration Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for adobe-firefly-integration as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Storing presigned URLs from the API instead of downloading immediately.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Generated image presigned URLs expire in approximately 1 hour. Always download immediately and write to stable storage.

failure-mode.description: Trusting empty outputs array as a success.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Content moderation blocks can return HTTP 200 with an empty outputs array. Always validate that outputs length is greater than 0 before treating a call as successful.

failure-mode.description: Passing user-provided freeform text directly to Firefly.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Unsanitized user input can trigger moderation blocks or inject unintended prompt content into a commercial asset pipeline. Sanitize and route through the pre-approved template only.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Firefly API rate limits (typically 10 req/s) require exponential backoff. Latency per generation is 2–8s; synchronous tight loops will exhaust limits and fail.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Credit consumption is opaque for automated pipelines; large batches exhaust allotments quickly. Monitor per-project with a dashboard and alert before budget exhaustion.
